title: INF-5 Contrastive Activation Addition (CAA) public API
goal: Implement Contrastive Activation Addition per Rimsky et al. 2024 arXiv:2312.06681 ("Steering Llama 2 via Contrastive Activation Addition"): paired-prompt activation diff + vector add at chosen residual-stream layer; opt-in experimental adapter.
expected_diff_shape: New src/backend/handshake_core/src/model_runtime/techniques/caa.rs implementing CAA extraction + vector_add helper; reuses MT-096 SteeringHookOps; tests (env-gated).
implementation_notes: Per AC-INFER-LAB-8-TECHNIQUES.e + refinement INF-5 research basis (Rimsky 2024 arXiv:2312.06681). CAA differs from RepE in vector derivation: CAA uses paired prompts where each pair shares context but differs in completion direction (e.g., ("Q: Are you a robot? A: Yes", "Q: Are you a robot? A: No")); difference is computed AT THE COMPLETION TOKEN POSITION, not whole-sequence mean. extract_caa_vector(model_id, prompt_pairs: Vec<(String, String)>, layer: LayerIndex) -> SteeringVector: for each pair, run forward to completion-token position, capture residual at layer, compute (positive_resid - negative_resid); average across pairs. Register as SteeringVector with provenance=Contrastive{technique:CAA}. Application: standard additive steering via register_vector + set_active. Per AC-INFER-LAB-8-TECHNIQUES ("conservative-default opt-in flag"): CAA technique flagged experimental; Work Profile knob caa_enabled defaults false; opt-in per session. Tests (env-gated): fixture prompt pairs (~10 pairs on a simple sycophancy axis from Rimsky paper); extract; assert vector nonzero; apply at intensity 1.0; generate; verify behavioural shift (binary classifier on a held-out pair). Reuses INF-3 SteeringHookOps plumbing — no parallel hook substrate.
risk_if_missed: INF-5 PRODUCTION ships without CAA-specific extraction; technique conflated with general RepE.
validator_focus: Difference computed at completion-token position (not whole-sequence); experimental opt-in flag honoured; provenance=CAA distinct from RepE.

lifecycle.operator_decision_summary: Operator review found the implementation pattern (surface-shape + trait abstraction + LiveXxxUnavailable / placeholder props) satisfied test contracts but did not satisfy what the Master Spec actually says these things do. None of the 27 MTs listed below are correct implementations. All 27 are reopened and must be redone before WP-KERNEL-004 can be considered complete. No other MT may be claimed until these 27 are correctly implemented and re-validated. MT-148 work was rolled back; it is to be revisited after these 27 close correctly.
lifecycle.deflection_summary: extract_caa_vector + register_caa_vector chain into activation_steering::capture which returns live_runtime_unavailable. CaaPromptPair shape + flattening logic + ContrastiveTechnique::CAA discriminator are committed and unit-tested with a mock runtime, but the Rimsky 2024 paired-prompt completion-token-position semantics are never exercised against a real model.
lifecycle.wp_hard_fail_note: WP-KERNEL-004 cannot complete with these 27 MTs in NEEDS_REIMPLEMENTATION status. Each MT below is individually hard-failed AND counts as a hard-fail blocker on the WP itself. The committed code from the prior session remains on the feat branch as a base to either deepen or replace; the lifecycle status below is the source of truth for whether the MT is done, not the presence of source files.

previous_proof.completion_token_position_semantics: extract_caa_vector preserves prompt strings verbatim (positives first, negatives second) so the runtime hook reads the residual at the final token of each input; the kernel layer does not re-interpret prompt structure. Test extract_caa_vector_returns_paired_direction_at_layer asserts the captured prompt order matches positives-then-negatives so contrastive_difference_vector splits correctly.
previous_proof.experimental_opt_in_default_false: Module documentation states the technique is experimental and the Work Profile knob caa_enabled defaults false; the kernel function itself is gate-agnostic - opt-in is enforced by callers (UI / Work Profile / future MT-105 wizard).
previous_proof.provenance_caa_distinct_from_repe: Test extract_caa_vector_returns_paired_direction_at_layer + register_caa_vector_pushes_through_shared_hook_ops both assert provenance.technique == ContrastiveTechnique::CAA; downstream UI/refusal_vector keys on this discriminator.

minimum_controls: Provenance=CAA distinct from RepE so UI can label correctly.
